revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ebisuyama Kofun]][[ja:蛭子山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Yosano, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ebisuyama Kofun | native_name =蛭子山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ebisuyama Kofun (Yosano) kouenbu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ebisuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ebisuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.507156|N|135.104514|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|qid=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto|1=Yosano, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|25.76|N|135|6|16.25|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Ebisuyama Kofun'''|蛭子山古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|qid=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto|1=Yosano, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1668 |title=蛭子山古墳 |trans-title= Ebisuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Ebisuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the Noda River basin in the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]], and is orientated to the northwest. The tumulus has an overall length of 170 meters, and is the largest of a group of eight tumuli in this vicinity. It is estimated to have been constructed in the middleof the Kofun period, or the early 4th century. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and had both ''[[fukiishi]]'', and cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. After damage due to the {{ill|1927 North Tango earthquake|en|1927 North Tango earthquake|qid=Q11400894|lt=1927 North Tango earthquake|1=1927 North Tango earthquake}}, the tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] and three burial facilities have been found in the center of the posterior circular portion, of which one contained a boat-shaped sarcophagus with elaborate carvings. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s and iron swords and spearheads. The sarcophagus was designated a Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> Currently, the site, together with the [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Nodagawa Station|Nodagawa Station]] on the {{ill|Miyazu Line|en|Miyazu Line|qid=Q6884480|lt=Miyazu Line|1=Miyazu Line}} of the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|qid=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway|1=Kyoto Tango Railway}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Total length: 170 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters wide x 11 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 100 meter diameter x 16 meters high, 3-tiers ==Gallery== <gallery> File:Ebisuyama Kofun (Yosano) funcho-1.JPG|Summit of posterior circular portion File:Ebisuyama Kofun (Yosano) funcho-2.JPG|Posterior looking towards anterior File:Ebisuyama Kofun (Yosano) funcho-3.JPG|Anterior looking towards posterior Ebisuyama Kofun (Yosano) sekkan.JPG|Sarcophagus 蛭子山1号墳出土 鉄槍.JPG|iron spearhead 蛭子山1号墳 出土埴輪群-1.JPG|Cylindrical ''haniwa'' 蛭子山1号墳 出土埴輪群-2.JPG|Cylindrical ''haniwa'' </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|qid=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:dd]]{{template-inline|qq=}} *[https://yosano-kankou.net/kankou/kofunpark/ Yosano Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Ebisuyama Kofun]]
